content:
- Finished the runtime-node startup-boundary task end to end.
- Final state: make check, make lint, make test, make test-long, and make docs-lint were all clean; task file marked completed/passes=true; task_switch ran; commit 9c399f9 pushed to origin/master.
- Docs note: added docs/src/explanation/process-management.md and linked it from SUMMARY after the K2 loop; K2 revise failed twice with tool-call artifacts so I did minimal factual cleanup manually after following the retry path.

- Verified that the task-finished commit 9c399f9 is on local master and already pushed to origin/master.
- Verified make check, make lint, make test, make test-long, and make docs-lint all pass in this workspace.
- Remaining local changes are only post-push Ralph bookkeeping (current task summary timestamps plus this session progress file), not task implementation deltas.

- Read prior progress log and scanned .ralph/current_tasks.md plus .ralph/current_tasks_done.md to choose the next task.
- Resolved blockers against done tasks: cert-reload is unblocked because task 08 is done; config-simplification remains blocked because story-ctl-operator-experience is not fully complete due to task 09 still active; postgres-role-reconciliation remains blocked by config-simplification.
- No active failing bug tasks exist. Among unblocked failing non-bug tasks, the highest priority visible candidate is .ralph/tasks/story-general-architecture-improvement-finding/04-task-remove-public-test-harness-from-the-production-library-surface-and-move-test-support-behind-a-dev-only-boundary.md because it is medium priority and unblocked, while the other unblocked tasks are low priority.
- Next step if context ended immediately: keep current_task pointed at the general-architecture task 04 and quit.

- Read the active Ralph task and confirmed it has no NOW EXECUTE marker, so this turn is the required design-first pass.
- Mapped current test_harness usage. Internal unit tests under src need crate-private support, while integration tests under tests currently consume the public export. That means a plain #[cfg(test)] private module is not enough.
- Chosen boundary design: rename src/test_harness to src/dev_support, make it pub(crate) for unit tests and feature-gated public only for a dedicated dev-only support crate; update integration tests to depend on that support crate instead of the production library surface.
- Next if context ends: apply the boundary/type rename broadly, add the support crate skeleton and task plan with NOW EXECUTE, then stop without fixing compile errors.

- Completed the design-only pass for task 04 and stopped before compile repair, as required.
- Moved src/test_harness to src/dev_support, removed the default public production export from src/lib.rs, and replaced it with a unit-test-private / feature-gated dev-support boundary.
- Added workspace member crates/pgtuskmaster_test_support as the planned integration-test-facing dev-only crate, and rewired tests/bdd_api_http.rs to import from that crate.
- Repointed in-crate unit-test/support references from crate::test_harness to crate::dev_support, updated the task file with a <plan> ending in NOW EXECUTE, and verified there are no remaining code references to test_harness::. 
- I did not repair compile errors or run make targets in this pass. Next step is the NOW EXECUTE pass: fix fallout, reduce visibility, run make check/lint/test/test-long, then docs.

- Read prior Ralph progress and confirmed current task points at task 04 remove-public-test-harness.
- Task file already ends with NOW EXECUTE, so this turn is the execution/repair pass rather than the design-only pass.
- Next I am collecting compile fallout from the dev_support + pgtuskmaster_test_support rewrite, then I need to fix visibility/import/build issues and run the required make targets.

- Confirmed the workspace compiles with make check after the dev_support boundary rewrite; no hard compile fallout remained.
- Current state is mostly cleanup/validation: warnings from dead code inside dev-only support, which may or may not matter for clippy depending on settings.
- Next I am running make lint, then the test targets, and I will only do docs after the full validation set is green.

- make lint initially failed because the orphan-file guard uses git ls-files and the deleted src/test_harness tree had not been staged out of the index yet.
- Staged the src/test_harness deletions with git add -u src/test_harness; rerunning make lint now.
- Assuming lint goes green, the remaining path is make test, make test-long, docs update if needed, then task bookkeeping and commit/push.

- make check and make lint are now green.
- Lint fix was precise: allow(dead_code) only for the cfg(test) dev_support module and the ApiObservedState enum in test builds, because those shapes exist specifically for dev/test support and were tripping clippy on the lib-test target.
- make test is running now; after that I still need make test-long, then docs evaluation/update, then task completion bookkeeping.

- make test-long initially failed because the HA Docker image build could not see the new workspace member.
- Fixed docker/Dockerfile to copy the dev-support crate manifest/source into planner and builder stages, then fixed .dockerignore so Docker build context includes crates/pgtuskmaster_test_support.
- Re-running make test-long now; setup is no longer failing immediately, so I am waiting on the HA suite.
